collections_sample.dataset_id: AMSR-E/Aqua L1A Raw Observation Counts V003
collections_sample.title: AMSR-E/Aqua L1A Raw Observation Counts V003
collections_sample.summary: AMSR-E Level-1A observation counts are processed from Level-0 science packet data by the Japan Aerospace Exploration Agency (JAXA) Earth Observation Center (EOC) in Japan.

collections_sample.dataset_id: AMSR-E/Aqua L2A Global Swath Spatially-Resampled Brightness Temperatures V004
collections_sample.title: AMSR-E/Aqua L2A Global Swath Spatially-Resampled Brightness Temperatures V004
collections_sample.summary: The AMSR-E Level-2A product (AE_L2A) contains daily 50 minute half-orbit swath brightness temperatures for six channels ranging from 6.9 GHz through 89 GHz. Data are resampled to spatial resolutions ranging from 5.4 km to 56 km. Each file is packaged with geolocation and quality information as well as ancillary data.

collections_sample.dataset_id: AMSR-E/Aqua L2B Global Swath Ocean Products derived from Wentz Algorithm V002
collections_sample.title: AMSR-E/Aqua L2B Global Swath Ocean Products derived from Wentz Algorithm V002
collections_sample.summary: This daily Level-2B swath data set includes Sea Surface Temperature (SST), Near-Surface Wind Speed, Columnar Water Vapor, and Cloud liquid Water data arrays, and was used as input to generate the following daily, weekly, and monthly Level-3 gridded ocean products;  AE_DyOcn, AE_WkOcn, and AE_MoOcn.

collections_sample.dataset_id: AMSR-E/Aqua L2B Global Swath Surface Precipitation GSFC Profiling Algorithm V003
collections_sample.title: AMSR-E/Aqua L2B Global Swath Surface Precipitation GSFC Profiling Algorithm V003
collections_sample.summary: The AMSR-E/Aqua Level-2B precipitation product includes instantaneous surface precipitation rate and type over ice-free/snow-free land and ocean between 89.24 degrees north and south latitudes at at 10 km spatial resolution along the track and 5 km spatial resolution along the scan. The data are generated by the GPROF 2010 Version 2 algorithm using Version 3 of the AMSR-E Level-2A Brightness Temperatures.

collections_sample.dataset_id: AMSR-E/Aqua L2B Global Swath Surface Precipitation GSFC Profiling Algorithm V004
collections_sample.title: AMSR-E/Aqua L2B Global Swath Surface Precipitation GSFC Profiling Algorithm V004
collections_sample.summary: The AMSR-E/Aqua Level-2B precipitation product includes instantaneous surface precipitation rate and type over ice-free/snow-free land and ocean between 89.24 degrees north and south latitudes at at 10 km spatial resolution along the track and 5 km spatial resolution along the scan. The data are generated by the GPROF 2017 algorithm using Version 4 of the AMSR-E Level-2A Brightness Temperatures.

collections_sample.dataset_id: AMSR-E/Aqua L2B Surface Soil Moisture, Ancillary Parms, & QC EASE-Grids V002
collections_sample.title: AMSR-E/Aqua L2B Surface Soil Moisture, Ancillary Parms, & QC EASE-Grids V002
collections_sample.summary: This Level-2B land surface product (AE_Land) includes daily measurements of surface soil moisture, vegetation/roughness water content interpretive information, and quality control variables. Ancillary data include time, geolocation, and quality assessment.

collections_sample.dataset_id: AMSR-E/Aqua L2B Surface Soil Moisture, Ancillary Parms, & QC EASE-Grids V003
collections_sample.title: AMSR-E/Aqua L2B Surface Soil Moisture, Ancillary Parms, & QC EASE-Grids V003
collections_sample.summary:
This data set contains gridded estimates of soil moisture in the top 1 cm of soil, averaged over the AMSR-E retrieval footprint. Soil moisture is estimated from AMSR-E/Aqua L2A brightness temperature (Tb) measurements using two different approaches: the Normalized Polarization Difference algorithm (NPD) and the Single Channel Algorithm (SCA).

Ancillary data are also provided to help interpret the soil moisture observations, including vegetation roughness, observation counts for various surfa...

collections_sample.dataset_id: AMSR-E/Aqua level 3 global monthly Surface Soil Moisture Averages V005 (AMSRE_AVRMO) at GES DISC
collections_sample.title: AMSR-E/Aqua level 3 global monthly Surface Soil Moisture Averages V005 (AMSRE_AVRMO) at GES DISC
collections_sample.summary: The dataset contains global monthly-mean soil moisture statistics (average values) for 1 by 1 degree grid cells. The source for the data is AMSR-E daily estimates of soil moisture (AE_Land3.002: AMSR-E/Aqua Daily L3 Surface Soil Moisture, Interpretive Parameters, QC EASE-Grids. Version 2 ). The dataset covers the time period from 2002-10-01 to 2011-09-30.

collections_sample.dataset_id: AMSR-E/Aqua level 3 global monthly Surface Soil Moisture Standard Deviation V005 (AMSRE_STDMO) at GES DISC
collections_sample.title: AMSR-E/Aqua level 3 global monthly Surface Soil Moisture Standard Deviation V005 (AMSRE_STDMO) at GES DISC
collections_sample.summary: The dataset contains global monthly soil moisture statistics (standard deviation ) for 1 by 1 degree grid cells. The source for the data is AMSR-E daily estimates of soil moisture (AE_Land3.002: AMSR-E/Aqua Daily L3 Surface Soil Moisture, Interpretive Parameters, QC EASE-Grids. Version 2 ). The dataset covers the time period from 2002-10-01 to 2011-09-30.

collections_sample.dataset_id: AMSR-E/Aqua Monthly Global Microwave Land Surface Emissivity, Version 1
collections_sample.title: AMSR-E/Aqua Monthly Global Microwave Land Surface Emissivity, Version 1
collections_sample.summary: This data set is a global land emissivity product using passive microwave observations from the Advanced Microwave Scanning Radiometer - Earth Observing System (AMSR-E). The data set complements existing land emissivity products from the Special Sensor Microwave Imager (SSM/I) and Advanced Microwave Sounding Unit (AMSU) by adding land emissivity estimates at two lower frequencies, 6.9 and 10.65 GHz (C- and X-band, respectively). Observations at these low frequencies penetrate deeper into the ...

collections_sample.dataset_id: AMSR-E/Aqua Monthly L3 5x5 deg Rainfall Accumulations V002
collections_sample.title: AMSR-E/Aqua Monthly L3 5x5 deg Rainfall Accumulations V002
collections_sample.summary: This Level-3 rainfall accumulation product (AE_RnGd) consists of two grids of 28 rows by 72 columns of monthly averaged rainfall accumulation over ocean and land. Both grids are 5 degree by 5 degree resolution.

collections_sample.dataset_id: AMSR-E/Aqua Monthly L3 Global Ascending/Descending .25x.25 deg Ocean Grids V002
collections_sample.title: AMSR-E/Aqua Monthly L3 Global Ascending/Descending .25x.25 deg Ocean Grids V002
collections_sample.summary: The Level-3 daily product (AE_DyOcn), weekly product (AE_WkOcn), and monthly product (AE_MoOcn) include SST, near-surface wind speed, columnar water vapor, and columnar cloud liquid water over oceans in a 0.25 degree by 0.25 degree grid, generated from AE_Ocean.

collections_sample.dataset_id: AMSR-E/Aqua Monthly L3 Global Snow Water Equivalent EASE-Grids V002
collections_sample.title: AMSR-E/Aqua Monthly L3 Global Snow Water Equivalent EASE-Grids V002
collections_sample.summary: These Level-3 Snow Water Equivalent (SWE) data sets contain SWE data and quality assurance flags mapped to Northern and Southern Hemisphere 25 km Equal-Area Scalable Earth Grids (EASE-Grids).

collections_sample.dataset_id: AMSR-E/Aqua surface soil moisture (LSMEM) L3 1 day 0.25 degree x 0.25 degree V001 (WC_LSMEM_SOILM_025) at GES DISC
collections_sample.title: AMSR-E/Aqua surface soil moisture (LSMEM) L3 1 day 0.25 degree x 0.25 degree V001 (WC_LSMEM_SOILM_025) at GES DISC
collections_sample.summary: AMSR-E/Aqua surface soil moisture (LSMEM) L3 1 day 0.25 degree x 0.25 degree V001 is a global, 10-year (2002-2011) data set. It is created from soil moisture retrieved from passive microwave brightness temperatures measured by the 10.65 and 36.5 GHz radiometers on the Advanced Microwave Scanning Radiometer-Earth Observing System (AMSR-E) sensor on the NASA Aqua satellite. The retrieval algorithm is based on Princeton's Land Surface Microwave Emission Model (LSMEM), a physically-based radiativ...

collections_sample.dataset_id: AMSR-E/Aqua Weekly L3 Global Ascending/Descending .25x.25 deg Ocean Grids V002
collections_sample.title: AMSR-E/Aqua Weekly L3 Global Ascending/Descending .25x.25 deg Ocean Grids V002
collections_sample.summary: The Level-3 daily product (AE_DyOcn), weekly product (AE_WkOcn), and monthly product (AE_MoOcn) include SST, near-surface wind speed, columnar water vapor, and columnar cloud liquid water over oceans in a 0.25 degree by 0.25 degree grid, generated from AE_Ocean.

collections_sample.dataset_id: AMSR-MODIS Boundary Layer Water Vapor L3 Daily 1 degree x 1 degree V1 (AMDBLWV) at GES DISC
collections_sample.title: AMSR-MODIS Boundary Layer Water Vapor L3 Daily 1 degree x 1 degree V1 (AMDBLWV) at GES DISC
collections_sample.summary: This data set provides an estimate the marine boundary layer water vapor beneath uniform cloud fields. Microwave radiometry from AMSR-E and AMSR-2 provides the total column water vapor, while the near-infrared imagery from MODIS provides the water vapor above the cloud layers. The difference between the two gives the vapor between the surface and the cloud top, which may be interpreted as the boundary layer water vapor.

collections_sample.dataset_id: AMSR/ADEOS-II L2A Global Swath Spatially-Resampled Brightness Temperatures V001
collections_sample.title: AMSR/ADEOS-II L2A Global Swath Spatially-Resampled Brightness Temperatures V001
collections_sample.summary: The AMSR Level-2A product (AA_L2A) contains brightness temperatures at 6.9 GHz, 10.65 GHz, 18.7 GHz, 23.8 GHz, 36.5 GHz, 89.0 GHz, 50.3 GHz, and 52.8GHz. Data are resampled to be spatially consistent except for the 50.3GHz and 52.8GHz data, and therefore are available at a variety of resolutions that correspond to the footprint sizes of the observations such as 56 km, 38 km, 24 km, 21 km, 12 km, and 5.4 km, respectively.

collections_sample.dataset_id: AMSR2 Daily Polar Gridded Brightness Temperatures, Version 2
collections_sample.title: AMSR2 Daily Polar Gridded Brightness Temperatures, Version 2
collections_sample.summary: The AMSR2 Daily Polar Gridded Brightness Temperatures product provides daily polar stereographic gridded daily brightness temperatures for both the Northern and Southern Hemispheres. As this product is intended to provide a follow-on data set for Near-Real-Time DMSP SSMIS Daily Polar Gridded Brightness Temperatures (NSIDC-0080), it uses TB fields gridded to match TBs gridded from SSMIS sensors on the same 25km polar stereo grids.

collections_sample.summary: The AMSR2 Daily Polar Gridded Sea Ice Concentrations data set provides sea ice concentrations for both the Northern and Southern Hemispheres. The Advanced Microwave Scanning Radiometer 2 (AMSR2) sensor on the Japan Aerospace Exploration Agency (JAXA) Global Change Observation Mission – Water (GCOM-W) satellite provides the daily passive microwave brightness temperature [TB] data via AMSR2 Daily Polar Gridded Brightness Temperatures (NSIDC-0802)  which is used as input for this product. collections_sample.dataset_id: AMSU/MSU Lowstratosphere Day/Month Temperature Anomalies and Annual Cycle
collections_sample.title: AMSU/MSU Lowstratosphere Day/Month Temperature Anomalies and Annual Cycle
collections_sample.summary: The AMSU/MSU Lowstratosphere Day/Month Temperature Anomalies and Annual Cycle V6 dataset consists of temperature anomalies and annual cycle temperatures derived from the Microwave Sounding Unit (MSU) and the Advanced Microwave Sounding Unit-A (AMSU-A) radiance data since January 1978. All products are derived for the lower stratosphere. The dataset begins on January 1, 1978 and is still currently ongoing. The data are available in netCDF-4 and ASCII formats.

collections_sample.title: AMSU/MSU Lowtroposphere Day/Month Temperature Anomalies and Annual Cycle V6

All products are derived for the lower troposphere. The dataset begins on January 1, 1978 and is still currently ongoing. The data are available in netCDF-4 and ASCII formats.

collections_sample.dataset_id: AMSU/MSU Midtroposphere Day/Month Temperature Anomalies and Annual Cycle V6
collections_sample.title: AMSU/MSU Midtroposphere Day/Month Temperature Anomalies and Annual Cycle V6
collections_sample.summary: The AMSU/MSU Midtroposphere Day/Month Temperature Anomalies and Annual Cycle V6 dataset consists of temperature anomalies and annual cycle temperatures derived from the Microwave Sounding Unit (MSU) and the Advanced Microwave Sounding Unit-A (AMSU-A) radiance data since January 1978. All products are derived for the mid-troposphere. The dataset begins on January 1, 1978 and is still currently ongoing. The data are available in netCDF-4 and ASCII formats.

collections_sample.dataset_id: AMSU/MSU Tropopause Day/Month Temperature Anomalies and Annual Cycle V6
collections_sample.title: AMSU/MSU Tropopause Day/Month Temperature Anomalies and Annual Cycle V6
collections_sample.summary: The AMSU/MSU Tropopause Day/Month Temperature Anomalies and Annual Cycle V6 dataset consists of temperature anomalies and annual cycle temperatures derived from the Microwave Sounding Unit (MSU) and the Advanced Microwave Sounding Unit-A (AMSU-A) radiance data since January 1978. All products are derived for the tropopause. The dataset begins on January 1, 1978 and is still currently ongoing. The data are available in netCDF-4 and ASCII formats.

collections_sample.dataset_id: Amundsen Sea CTD data
collections_sample.title: Amundsen Sea CTD data
collections_sample.summary: Preliminary CTD and LADCP data from Oden Southern Ocean 2008/2009

collections_sample.dataset_id: Amundsen Sea Sector Data Set
collections_sample.title: Amundsen Sea Sector Data Set
collections_sample.summary: This data set includes a nested model, that starts at low resolution for the whole Antarctic Ice Sheet, and then embeds higher resolution data at limited domains. There are at least three levels of nesting: whole, regional, and specific ice streams. Investigators focused on the Thwaites Glacier and the Pine Island Glacier. The model was produced using data from (Holt et al. 2006) and (Vaughan et al. 2006). Data are in Network Common Data Form (NetCDF) format and are available via FTP.

collections_sample.dataset_id: Amundsen Sea Swath Bathymetry
collections_sample.title: Amundsen Sea Swath Bathymetry
collections_sample.summary:
Swath bathymetric mapping of the Amundsen Sea continental shelf margin from 122
      degrees 15' W to 102 W, and made 5 additional crossings west of 122 degrees 15'
      W, and coverage close to the coast of all embayments west of Thwaites Glacier,
      and detailed coverage of the area immediately north and east of Thwaites
      Glacier.

collections_sample.dataset_id: An account of the establishment of Macquarie Island Station in 1948-1949 by Peter Wylie King
collections_sample.title: An account of the establishment of Macquarie Island Station in 1948-1949 by Peter Wylie King
collections_sample.summary:
This file contains 22 pages of scanned typewritten text, and 1 page of a sketched map.  The document describes the establishment of Macquarie Island station in 1948, but the first ANARE (Australian National Antarctic Research Expeditions) party.  It was written by Peter Wylie King on October 20, 1951.

collections_sample.dataset_id: An Analysis of the Zooplankton Community Structure of the Western Beaufort Sea. WEBSEC 1971
collections_sample.title: An Analysis of the Zooplankton Community Structure of the Western Beaufort Sea. WEBSEC 1971
collections_sample.summary: The Beaufort Sea, north of the North Slope Oil Field in Alaska, is an area in which the likelihood of future oil pollution is great. As an attempt to assess the effects of this pollution upon the marine environment, the United States Coast Guard Oceanographic Unit sampled an area from 143 30 to 154 30 west longitude between 70 and 72 north Latitude, in the Western Beaufort Sea, from August to mid September 1971. collections_sample.dataset_id: An annotated library of underwater acoustic recordings for testing and training automated algorithms for detecting Antarctic blue and fin whale sounds
collections_sample.title: An annotated library of underwater acoustic recordings for testing and training automated algorithms for detecting Antarctic blue and fin whale sounds
collections_sample.summary:
This annotated library contains both a data set and a data product. 

The data set contains a sub-sample of underwater recordings made around Antarctica from 2005-2017. These recordings were curated and sub-sampled from a variety of national and academic recording campaigns. Recordings were made using a variety of different instruments, and sub-samples span 11 different combinations of site and year. Spatial coverage of the recordings includes sites in the Western Antarctic Peninsula, Atlanti...

collections_sample.dataset_id: An annual cycle of cloud condensation nuclei concentration in the Ross Sea Region
collections_sample.title: An annual cycle of cloud condensation nuclei concentration in the Ross Sea Region
collections_sample.summary: A cycle of cloud condensation nuclei concentration in the Ross Sea Region was collected by installing and running a CCN monitor at Scott Base. The results contributed to the Southern Hemisphere record of CCN which is needed to establish the relationship between clouds and the sulphur cycle.

collections_sample.dataset_id: An artificial substrate experiment to study the colonisation of new areas of sediment in terrestrial aquatic ecosystems, Lake Hoare
collections_sample.title: An artificial substrate experiment to study the colonisation of new areas of sediment in terrestrial aquatic ecosystems, Lake Hoare
collections_sample.summary: An artificial substrate experiment was designed to mimic the colonisation of new areas of sediment that are introduced to Lake Hoare by episodic and localised slippage of accumulations of sediment through the ice cover.  These appear on the lake floor as sediment mounds.  At the Lake Hoare, experimental sediments were introduced as pre-prepared frozen "sediment burgers". collections_sample.dataset_id: An assessment of variability in the influx of cosmic dust during the Holocene and the potential effect on iron concentrations in the Southern Ocean.
collections_sample.title: An assessment of variability in the influx of cosmic dust during the Holocene and the potential effect on iron concentrations in the Southern Ocean.
collections_sample.summary:
Metadata record for data from AAS (ASAC) project 3132.

Public 
This research will determine variability in the influx and mineralogy of cosmic dust to the Southern Ocean during the Holocene from peat bog cores. Cosmic dust contains significant quantities of soluble iron, a micronutrient required for photosynthesis. Therefore, variations in the deposition of cosmic dust could significantly affect primary production in the Southern Ocean. This may also play an important role in global climate ...

collections_sample.title: An Assessment of Volcanic Threat and Monitoring Capabilities in the United States: Framework for a National Volcano Early Warning System
collections_sample.summary:
A National Volcano Early Warning System NVEWS is being formulated by the
